task_completion.description: Forward at least two security-related emails to the security team.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 'Sent' folder or active compose windows for at least two emails forwarded to 'security-team@techcorp.org'. The body of these emails should contain security-related keywords (e.g., spam, filter, rule, phishing).

task_completion.description: Forward at least two development-related emails to the development team.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 'Sent' folder or active compose windows for at least two emails forwarded to 'dev-team@techcorp.org'. The body of these emails should contain development-related keywords (e.g., patch, bug, code, commit).

task_completion.description: Forward at least one community/general email to the community lead.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 'Sent' folder or active compose windows for at least one email forwarded to 'community-lead@techcorp.org'. The body should contain community-related keywords (e.g., linux, meeting, event, social).

task_completion.description: Compose a summary email to the director with the correct subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 'Sent' folder or an open compose window for an email addressed to 'director@techcorp.org' with the exact subject 'Handoff Summary'.

task_completion.description: Include the delegated teams in the summary email body.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the body of the 'Handoff Summary' email. It must explicitly mention the teams that tasks were delegated to (e.g., Security, Development, Community) or list their email addresses.

task_completion.description: Do not delete or move original emails from the inbox.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main Inbox view. It should still be populated with the original emails. There should be no evidence of emails being moved to 'Trash', 'Archive', or custom folders.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical email client to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show an actual email client (like Thunderbird or Evolution) being used to read, forward, and compose emails, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Forwarded emails contain actual content from the inbox.
integrity.visual_evidence: The forwarded emails in the 'Sent' folder should show the original email headers and body from the inbox, not manually typed mock text or empty bodies.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the email client by directly editing Maildir files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows showing text editors (like nano or vim) or shell commands (like echo or cp) being used to directly create or modify files in the Maildir structure.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target recipients and minimum counts: security-team@techcorp.org (>= 2 emails), dev-team@techcorp.org (>= 2 emails), community-lead@techcorp.org (>= 1 email), and director@techcorp.org (for the summary). Security keywords to look for in forwarded emails: spam, filter, rule, score, bayes, blackhole, abuse, phishing. Development keywords: patch, bug, code, commit, diff, release, feature, widget, tcl. Community keywords: linux, meeting, event, user group, ilug, announce, party, social.
